name: Magnitude
maintenance_status: Maintained but lower-velocity as of 2026-07-09. The public TypeScript repository is not archived, has about 604 commits and 4,100 stars, and was last pushed on 2026-02-08. Material post-January-2025 work includes Playwright/CDP access, visual assertions, provider configuration, prompt caching, parallel workers, and a February 2026 token-accounting fix.
license: Apache-2.0 for the open-source framework, compatible with ApplyPilot's AGPL-3.0 codebase when license and notice obligations are preserved. Selected model providers and any enterprise support remain subject to separate terms.
pricing_model: No Magnitude framework fee for self-hosting. Marginal cost is the chosen visually grounded LLM's token pricing plus local or hosted browser compute. Magnitude defaults to Claude Sonnet 4 when an Anthropic key is present and also supports Qwen2.5-VL through OpenAI-compatible providers, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Bedrock, Azure, and local endpoints.
marginal_cost_per_attempt: [uncertain] No official ATS step/token benchmark exists. A planning range of $0.05-$0.50 in model cost per multi-step attempt plus browser compute is plausible for a screenshot-heavy Sonnet-class route, but must not be budgeted as fact. Instrument Magnitude's tokensUsed events and provider bills on synthetic and live canaries.
projected_cost_per_verified_apply: [uncertain] At hypothetical $0.05-$0.50 attempt cost and no fallback or labor, ApplyPilot's historical rates imply $0.15-$1.48 for Ashby at 33.7%, $0.17-$1.75 for Greenhouse at 28.6%, $0.60-$6.02 for other hosts at 8.3%, and $5-$50 for Workday at 1.0%. These are sensitivity bounds, not measured Magnitude results.
billing_granularity: Model billing follows the selected provider's tokens, requests, cache reads/writes, or local compute. Browser compute is local process time or the attached CDP/browser vendor's unit. Magnitude itself has no documented per-run fee. [uncertain] Minimum provider units, rate limits, and idle charges vary by deployment.
model_cost: [uncertain] Magnitude requires strong instruction following and visual grounding, recommends Claude Sonnet 4, and documents Qwen2.5-VL-72B as a cheaper/open alternative. Anthropic prompt caching is enabled by default and Magnitude reports typically about 40% fewer tokens than uncached, but ATS input/output/cache counts and current provider prices must be measured and joined from bills.
infrastructure_cost: [uncertain] Local execution needs Node.js, Playwright Chromium, browser RAM/CPU, screenshot/video storage, and a model endpoint. Self-hosting Qwen2.5-VL 32B-72B generally requires substantial GPU memory or quantization, while remote models shift cost to tokens. Existing ApplyPilot Chrome workers can reduce incremental browser cost if CDP attachment is stable.
human_recovery_cost: [uncertain] Magnitude exposes Playwright Page and BrowserContext, so an application can pause and hand control to an operator, but no packaged HITL billing or takeover workflow is documented. At $50/hour, five minutes is $4.17; a 5% recovery rate adds about $0.21 per attempt. Measure auth, OTP, and ambiguous outcomes.
adapter_maintenance_tco: [uncertain] Planning estimate: 4-8 engineer-weeks because ApplyPilot is Python while Magnitude is TypeScript, requiring a service/IPC boundary, lifecycle supervision, model/provider management, profile locking, restricted tools, deterministic submit/verification, and telemetry joins; then 8-20 hours/month for framework, model, and site drift.
verified_completion_rate: [uncertain] The repository claims 94% on WebVoyager, but no official ATS-stratified job-application benchmark with positive submit evidence exists. Visual test assertions and generic browser-task success do not establish ApplyPilot completion. Credit no production completion until repeated live canaries prove ATS/network/DOM evidence.
failure_modes: Visual grounding misses, wrong coordinates after layout shift, stale screenshot, planning/instruction failure, context growth, token/rate limit, Playwright or CDP disconnect, profile lock, popup/tab confusion, cross-origin iframe, file upload, auth/OTP/challenge, conditional required fields, visual assertion false positives, unsupported question, and ambiguous submit after crash.
crash_recovery: Playwright artifacts, narration, token events, screenshots, video, and direct Page/Context hooks can aid diagnosis. Tests can fail fast or continue. Magnitude does not document transactional action replay or mid-submit resume; restart only from a pre-submit checkpoint and quarantine any crash after a submit-touching action.
exactly_once_submit_safety: No built-in job deduplication or exactly-once third-party submit primitive exists. Magnitude's act and visual checks are not idempotency controls. ApplyPilot must intercept or remove generic submit clicks and expose one deterministic submit command with lease, job/account dedup, plan hash, one-time authorization, response capture, and ambiguity quarantine.
verification_evidence_tier: Magnitude provides natural-language visual checks plus direct Playwright Page/Context access for DOM, network, cookies, and browser instrumentation. Use deterministic network/DOM extraction to capture ATS/application ID or successful response as primary evidence; visual checks and screenshots are secondary and agent/test pass state alone is insufficient.
verification_latency: [uncertain] Direct Playwright response or DOM checks can be synchronous; visual checks require an additional model round trip and email remains delayed. No ATS-specific latency distribution exists. Capture browser action, model, verification, and delayed reconciliation separately.
headless_headed_parity: [uncertain] Magnitude can run anywhere Playwright runs, supports local browser launch and CDP attachment, and CI examples use Xvfb. Visual models may be sensitive to viewport, fonts, rendering, challenge behavior, and virtual-screen scaling. ATS parity across headed, headless, and attached user-controlled Chrome is unmeasured.
python_integration: Weak to moderate. Magnitude is TypeScript/Node-first, whereas ApplyPilot is Python. Integration requires a sidecar process, JSON-RPC/HTTP/CLI adapter, or reimplementation of route contracts. Direct in-process Python support is not documented.
playwright_cdp_reuse: Strong on the Node side. startBrowserAgent accepts browser.cdp to connect to an open CDP-enabled browser, launchOptions can enable a debugging port, and agent.page/context expose the underlying Playwright objects. ApplyPilot needs exclusive tab/profile ownership and must avoid simultaneous Python and Magnitude control.
migration_effort: [uncertain] Large for a production route because of the Python-to-TypeScript boundary and missing packaged safety/verification controls. A read-only visual comparator can be prototyped quickly, but final-submit use requires a supervised sidecar, answer-plan contract, profile/OTP handling, evidence schema, and ATS regression suite.
ats_fit: [uncertain] Vision-first interaction may help visually complex long-tail forms and visual assertions, but stable Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, and Workable routes should remain deterministic-first. Coordinate-based control is exposed to responsive layout and challenge drift. Workday is not justified without targeted proof.
deterministic_coverage: Magnitude exposes ordinary Playwright Page/Context operations, so known navigation, cookies, network interception, field filling, and verification can remain deterministic around model-assisted acts. Its advertised native deterministic caching was described as in progress. [uncertain] ApplyPilot's no-model share and stable replay yield are unmeasured.
escape_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. Measure jobs escaping because the model cannot ground a control, the plan exceeds cost/step limits, a required field is unsupported, auth/OTP/challenge appears, provenance fails, positive verification is absent, or human review is required. Do not chain a second agent after submit.
control_plane_overhead: [uncertain] Each reasoning loop uses the current screenshot, retained screenshots, action/thought history, and model round trips. Magnitude retains one screenshot by default plus cached screenshots and the last 20 thoughts; context grows roughly linearly. Capture pixels, encoded bytes, input/output/cache tokens, actions, retries, and wall time.
observability_joinability: Moderate. Narration, tokensUsed telemetry, test duration/token telemetry, Playwright Page/Context instrumentation, screenshots, optional video, and provider billing can be joined through a sidecar-supplied attempt_id. Magnitude does not provide ApplyPilot-native queue, email, or result-event joins; build a stable event envelope and hash artifacts.
persistent_profiles: [uncertain] Standard Playwright context options and CDP attachment allow cookies or an existing dedicated Chrome profile, but Magnitude docs do not define profile locking, cloning, portability, clean shutdown, or browser-version migration. ApplyPilot must own those lifecycle rules outside Magnitude.
existing_session_attach: Documented first-class CDP attachment through browser.cdp, plus direct access to the active Page and BrowserContext. Attach only to a dedicated ApplyPilot profile/tab after a health probe; do not allow concurrent controllers or attach to a user's general browsing profile.
login_and_otp: [uncertain] Existing CDP sessions can carry authenticated state, and direct Playwright access permits custom pause/OTP code, but Magnitude has no documented managed OTP relay or human-takeover product. Keep sensitive values out of model-visible prompts, use owner-controlled single-use codes, and verify login before resuming.
extension_support: [uncertain] Playwright launchOptions can pass Chromium arguments and an attached Chrome may have reviewed extensions, but Magnitude documentation does not establish extension lifecycle, MV3 service-worker reliability, content-script isolation, or native messaging support. Test any extension-dependent path explicitly.
session_data_boundary: Self-hosted browser state, screenshots, and artifacts can remain on the worker, but screenshots, prompts, history, and data sent to a remote model leave the machine under that provider's terms. The test runner collects basic anonymized duration and token telemetry by default unless telemetry:false. A local compatible model avoids model egress but adds GPU operations.
required_field_completeness: Visual acts and checks can reason about rendered forms, but they do not guarantee a complete inventory of visible, conditional, off-screen, or iframe required controls. Add deterministic DOM/accessibility and validity scans before planning and submit, trigger conditional sections, and refuse unknown required fields.
answer_provenance: Magnitude accepts structured data passed to act and custom prompts, but does not enforce source provenance. Use a signed ApplyPilot answer plan and deterministic lookup/fill bridge carrying source IDs; prohibit the model from inventing employment, demographic, authorization, or compensation facts.
unsupported_question_fallback: Wrap each act in an ApplyPilot policy layer that classifies unknown, sensitive, demographic, legal, work-authorization, compensation, and unsupported free text. Stop the sidecar with needs_review and hand the dedicated browser to an owner; never let a visual model guess and continue to submit.
prompt_injection_resistance: [uncertain] Magnitude's vision-first model sees page content and supports custom prompts, but primary docs do not publish domain allowlists, hostile-page evaluation, secret redaction, or a prompt-injection guarantee. Restrict navigation and exposed Playwright operations in the sidecar, isolate credentials, and test malicious visual/text instructions and cross-origin content.
site_permission_and_terms: [uncertain] The Apache framework license does not authorize automation on ATS or employer sites, and selected model/provider terms also apply. Review each interface and account policy, use only user-authorized flows, honor access controls, and do not deploy vision control to evade challenges or anti-automation measures.
irreversible_action_guard: Magnitude can click any visible control through act, so the sidecar must block generic interaction with recognized submit elements. Only a deterministic ApplyPilot submit RPC may cross the boundary after lease, provenance, completeness, dedup, host policy, and fresh page-signature checks. Start in no-submit observation mode.
concurrency: The test runner supports parallel workers through --workers/-w. [uncertain] Practical concurrency is bounded by browser memory, model rate and token limits, GPU capacity for local models, profile/account locks, and screenshot bandwidth. One worker must never share an authenticated profile concurrently.
startup_latency: [uncertain] Local Node and Chromium startup plus first model request are not benchmarked for ApplyPilot. CDP attachment should reduce warm-browser startup, while local 32B-72B model loading can dominate cold start. Measure sidecar spawn, browser/CDP connect, page ready, and first action separately.
warm_session_reuse: A BrowserAgent can retain its Page/Context across multiple acts, and CDP attachment can reuse an existing browser. Safely reuse only after clean-tab reset, profile lock, storage/download cleanup, health probe, model-cost meter reset, and an idle timeout to prevent cross-job leakage.
tracing_and_replay: Playwright access supports screenshots, network listeners, routes, and configured recordVideo; narration and test results retain semantic steps. Native deterministic caching was described as in progress, so no production replay guarantee exists. Capture Playwright trace/HAR in the wrapper and promote stable sequences to deterministic ApplyPilot code.
deployment_modes: Local Node/TypeScript library and test runner, CI anywhere Playwright runs, container or self-hosted sidecar, local or remote LLM providers including OpenAI-compatible endpoints, local or remote CDP browser, and hybrid deterministic Playwright plus model-assisted acts. No Magnitude-managed browser cloud is documented.
browser_version_matrix: Current implementation and examples center on Playwright Chromium and CDP. [uncertain] Firefox, WebKit, Safari, and WebDriver BiDi are not documented as Magnitude execution targets. Pin Magnitude, Playwright, Chromium, viewport, and fonts, then rerun ATS fixtures on upgrades.
canary_stop_loss: Enforce limits in the ApplyPilot sidecar: domain allowlist, maximum acts/model calls/tokens/wall time/dollars, one pre-submit retry, no post-submit retry, profile lock, and artifact capture. Trip immediately on duplicate submit, provenance violation, or secret exposure; quarantine missing confirmation and stop an ATS route when p95 cost or completion misses control.
benchmark_design: Compare deterministic adapters, current fallback, Magnitude with Sonnet, and Magnitude with a cheaper/local grounded model on matched ATS-stratified tasks. Include synthetic conditional fields, responsive layouts, visual-only controls, uploads, iframes, hostile content, auth, crash, and confirmation. Use repeated runs and unique live jobs with positive evidence.
route_funnel: Track eligibility, sidecar started, CDP/profile acquired, auth valid, deterministic inventory, visual route selected, acts/model calls/tokens, plan complete, provenance complete, submit guard approved, submit RPC called, network/DOM evidence, fallback before submit, human recovery, ambiguous quarantine, delayed email reconciliation, and final disposition.
step_context_budget: [uncertain] Initial canary: at most 12 model acts, 60,000 input-equivalent tokens, 6,000 output tokens, three retained screenshots, five minutes, and $0.20 model budget before fallback; no generic visual act may touch submit. Tune from measured token events and provider invoices.
cache_replay_yield: [uncertain] Anthropic prompt caching reportedly cuts Magnitude token use by about 40%, but that is context caching, not no-model action replay. Native deterministic run caching was described as in progress. Measure prompt-cache savings separately from stable form-signature replay promoted into deterministic adapters.
confidence_intervals: [uncertain] No ApplyPilot confidence interval exists. Report Wilson 95% intervals for positive completion, fallback, human recovery, and ambiguity by ATS/model/mode; bootstrap provider cost and latency per verified apply. The claimed WebVoyager score is not a prior for job submissions.
historical_bucket_impact: [uncertain] Magnitude may recover visually unusual long-tail browser failures, but it adds model/screenshot overhead and does not reduce preflight/policy failures. Low historical Workday and miscellaneous-host success can overwhelm cheap attempt pricing. Replay ApplyPilot buckets with measured completion, token, fallback, and human rates before promotion.
recommendation: WATCH, then run a small no-submit comparator canary. Magnitude's visual assertions, direct Playwright/CDP access, and provider flexibility are useful research controls, but the TypeScript boundary, unproven ATS completion, February 2026 last push, and missing packaged safety/HITL controls make it weaker than Python-native candidates for immediate production routing.
